urology.tagline: Urology & Endourology, Minimally Invasive Urinary Stone Surgery, Laparoscopic and Robotic Surgery
urology.brief_profile: Dr. Abdulaziz Alathel is a Consultant and an Assistant Professor of Urology, Endourology, Robotic and minimally invasive Surgery. He began his education at King Saud University's College of Medicine, where he earned a degree with honors. He subsequently joined the Saudi Board of Urology at King Abdulaziz Medical City -Ministry of National Guard, where he successfully completed his training and received recognition as the best resident with distinctive performance. Additionally, he held the position of chief resident at his training facility and for the entire central region under the auspices of the Saudi Commission for Health Specialties.(SCFHS). After that, he joined the University of Western Ontario (Canada) where he did a two year fellowship in Endourology, Minimally Invasive and Robotic Surgery, where he was awarded 2nd best research from the Society of Endourology during his time as a fellow. Dr. Alathel has been appointed by King Saud bin Abdulaziz University for Health Sciences -National Guard Hospital as an Assistant Professor of Urology in the College of Medicine. He takes an active role in educating and instructing medical trainees. His numerous papers in highly regarded peer-reviewed journals and numerous presentations at national and international conferences demonstrate his active involvement in medical research in the areas of urology and medical education. He is an active member in the American Urology Association (AUA), The European Association of Urology (EAU) and the Saudi Urological Association (SUA). Dr. Alathel treats a wide variety of urological conditions and has an additional specialized training in Minimally invasive surgery and Robotic surgery as well as a personal interest in treating urinary stones in both medical and surgical methods. He currently occupies multiple positions in different organizations such as, Specialized clinics director, Deputy program director for the urology residency training program at the National Guard Hospital. He is also an active member of the SCFHS Urology exam committee.
core_competencies: Medical and Surgical Management of Urinary stones - Utilizing rigid and flexible ureteroscopies with laser technology as well as percutaneous nephrolithotomy (PCNL) and extracorporeal shockwave lithotripsy (ESWL)

Medical and Surgical Management of all types of urinary incontinence in males and females

Management of chronic prostatitis and chronic pelvic pain syndrome (CPPS)

urology.brief_profile: Dr. Meshari Al Zahrani, MD,is a graduate from College of Medicine, Taif University, Saudi Arabia. He received his Urology residency training at the King Abdulaziz Medical City -Ministry of the National Guard in 2019 followed by a double fellowship in Andrology& Male Infertility from King Saud University and from Saudi Commission forHealth Specialties in 2021. Dr. Meshari Al Zahrani has been practicing as a Urology, Andrology, and Male Infertility consultant physician since 2022 practicing healthcare related to men’s health and male reproductive health. Dr. Al Zahrani is currently an Assistant Professor of Urology in the College of Medicine at Majmaah University involved in the teaching and training of medical students. He has also been involved in medical and clinical research with several publications at conferences or at journals in international peer-reviewed journals. He also participated and invited as a reviewer in several medical journals. He is currently appointed as the supervisor of the Medical student research club at the College of Medicine, the Editorial Board Committee, and the Managing Editor of the Majmaah Journal of Health Sciences. He is also an active member of different health and medical societies such as the Saudi Society of Men's Health and the Saudi Urological Association. Dr. AL Zahrani’s clinical and surgical interests are in the care, diagnosis and treatment of adult male patients with erectile dysfunction, Peyronie's disease, congenital penile curvature, sexually transmitted diseases, men sexual health, male infertility, obstructive and non-obstructive azoospermia, Also, penile prosthesis implant surgery, testicular prosthesis implant, surgical treatment of male infertility such as testicular sperm aspiration (TESA), testicular sperm extraction (TESE),microscopic testicular sperm extraction (microTESE), microscopic varicocelectomy, also, microsurgical reconstruction of the male genital tract such as vasovasostomy, vasoepididymostomy.

He then joined the Saudi Board of Urology at King Abdulaziz Medical City – Ministry of National Guard and finished his training with distinctive performance as he was rewarded the best resident during his training. internal_medicine.brief_profile: Dr. Mohammad Kurdi is a senior consultant in interventional cardiology. He is one of the leading interventional cardiologist and regional experts in the treatment of systemic hypertension by renal artery ablation (denervation) therapy. In addition, and to support he next generation of medical excellence, Dr. Kurdi has a long history of contribution in the fields of medical education and physician and technician training through his professional journey where he started at Prince Sultan Military Hospital (Riyadh) and later in Canada between Dalhousie University and Ottawa University (Ottawa Heart Institute) where he finished his cardiac and interventional cardiology programs. In 2003, Dr. Kurdi joined King Saud University (KSU) Medical School as a teaching staff and was the director of the Cardiac Intervention Laboratory. After 4 years at KSU, he was tasked to be part of the commissioning team for King Fahad Medical City and more specifically King Salman Heart Centre, where he served as chairman of cardiology for 3 years.Since 2012, Dr. Kurdi has been dedicated to private practice, where he developed the Cardiac centre with multiple private groups and hospitals and has been a major contributor in development of private cardiac services across the Kingdom. Given his long and experienced career, Dr. Kurdi is one of the leaders in cardiac services in the Kingdom, with a distinguished track record of practical experience, education, and implementation at the highest international standards of health care. He has also played a pivotal role as one of the founders of the Saudi Society for Cardiac Intervention and the Gulf Intervetional Society as a board member and founder. He has been recognized at the regional and international level as one of the key voices in the region, having served as speaker, faculty and board member for the multiple regional societies including main conferences in Asia and Europe.

internal_medicine.brief_profile: Dr. Rani Abu Khuzam, MD, EMHCL is a graduate of the American University of Beirut (AUB) Medical School. He received his Internal Medicine residency training at the American University of Beirut - Medical Center (AUBMC) in 2010 followed by a fellowship in Pulmonary & Critical Care Medicine at AUBMC in 2013 where he also received the Fellow of the Year award in 2012. Dr. Rani Abu Khuzam has been practicing as a Pulmonary and Intensive Care consultant physician since 2013 caring for critically ill patients in addition to practicing outpatient and in-patient pulmonary medicine at Ain Wazein Medical Village (AWMV), a 170-bed tertiary teaching hospital. Dr. Abu Khuzam was also a lecturer in the Lebanese University Medical School involved in the teaching and training of medical students, internal medicine residents and pulmonary/critical care fellows, and was also appointed as a Clinical Associate in the American University of Beirut Medical Center (AUB-MC). He has also been involved in clinical research with publications in international peer-reviewed journals. In 2016 Dr. Abu Khuzam was appointed Head of the Intensive Care Unit at AUB Medical Center. Dr. Abu Khuzam’s clinical interests are in the care of adult patients with Interstitial Lung Disease/Fibrosis, Asthma, COPD, pleural diseases, lung ultrasonography, bronchoscopic endobronchial ultrasonography (EBUS-FNA), critical care hemodynamics and fluid management.

cardiology.brief_profile: Dr. Hussam Faleh Al-Faleh is a consultant in cardiovascular medicine and a professor in the Department of Cardiac Sciences at the College of Medicine, King Saud University.  He graduated from King Saud University, then completed his training in Internal Medicine and Cardiology at the university of Alberta in Canada, earning the Canadian Fellowship and the American Board certification in both specialties. He further subspecialized in Interventional Cardiology, obtaining a fellowship from Dalhousie University in Canada, and a fellowship in Advanced Cardiac Echocardiography from the University of Ottawa in Canada.  He has worked as a consultant at King Saud University Medical City since 2005. During his tenure, he held several administrative roles including head of the Cardiac Catheterization Lab and the Adult Cardiology Unit. He also supervised the Saudi Fellowship Training Program in Adult Cardiovascular Diseases at King Saud University. He served as President of the Saudi Heart Association, the leading scientific body for all adult and pediatric cardiac medicine and surgery specialties, and was a board member prior to that. He has been involved in several national projects related to heart diseases, including leading the team that developed the first national guidelines for treating acute coronary syndrome. These guidelines were adopted by the Saudi Heart Association, the Saudi Health Council, and the Council of Health Insurance. He also led a team that developed certification criteria for designating healthcare facilities as Centers of Excellence for Acute Coronary Syndrome treatment, which were implemented in several institutions. He has also contributed to the health transformation initiative within the Ministry of Health and continues to be actively involved. Dr. Al-Faleh has authored and co-authored over 55 peer-reviewed scientific publications. His research interests include acute coronary syndrome (particularly in patients in Saudi Arabia), heart failure, and valvular heart diseases.

After 4 years at KSU, he was tasked to be part of the commissioning team for King Fahad Medical City and more specifically King Salman Heart Centre, where he served as chairman of cardiology for 3 years.Since 2012, Dr. Kurdi has been dedicated to private practice, where he developed the Cardiac centre with multiple private groups and hospitals and has been a major contributor in development of private cardiac services across the Kingdom. Given his long and experienced career, Dr. Kurdi is one of the leaders in cardiac services in the Kingdom, with a distinguished track record of practical experience, education, and implementation at the highest international standards of health care. He has also played a pivotal role as one of the founders of the Saudi Society for Cardiac Intervention and the Gulf Intervetional Society as a board member and founder. He has been recognized at the regional and international level as one of the key voices in the region, having served as speaker, faculty and board member for the multiple regional societies including main conferences in Asia and Europe.

vascular_surgery.brief_profile: Dr. Wesam Talal Abuznadah is a Consultant in Vascular and Endovascular Surgery with over 15 years of experience. Dr. Abuznadah has a special focus on varicose vein management from the cosmetic and therapeutic perspectives. He obtained his Bachelor of Medicine and Surgery degree from King Abdulaziz University in Jeddah in 2000. He then pursued postgraduate studies in Canada and obtained certifications in General Surgery and Vascular Surgery from the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada. After his studies, he joined the National Guard Health Affairs in Jeddah to lead the vascular surgery practice there. Currently he serves as the Governor of the American College of Surgeons Saudi Arabia Chapter.

pediatric_surgery.brief_profile: Prof. Almuhaizea is one of the founding pediatric neurologists in Saudi Arabia. With more than two decades of dedication to practice, medical education and research. He is the founding chair for the Saudi pediatric neurology training program and currently chairs its examination committee. He is also a founding member of the Saudi pediatric neurology society. He is a professor of pediatric neurology at Alfaisal university. His wide areas of interest span disorders of peripheral nervous system, movement disorders, neurogenetic disorders in children and neurodisability.‍
